intro: Amplitude and Heap both show up under the product analytics category, but they solve slightly different jobs. Amplitude uses pricing that starts at $61/mo (Plus) with free tier, while Heap runs on subscription from $1.7. Both sit at moderate lock-in. Transparency lands at 4/5 versus 3/5. Amplitude fits teams working on enterprise product analytics with advanced cohorts, while Heap is a closer match when the job is understanding user behavior (funnels, retention). Worth noting: Amplitude is explicitly not for startup with <1K users; Heap is explicitly not for simple projects. The honest trade-off: Amplitude trades off on startup with <1K users — PostHog free tier is more generous; Heap trades off on pre-product stage. On the plus side, Amplitude highlights enterprise product analytics with advanced cohorts, while Heap points to understanding user behavior (funnels, retention). Amplitude's documentation also calls out warehouse-native mode (Snowflake/BigQuery). Heap similarly notes data-driven product decisions. For teams evaluating product analytics options today, the right choice usually comes down to pricing model, lock-in, and which tool's stated use-case maps to yours.
quick_take: Amplitude is for enterprise product analytics with advanced cohorts; Heap is for understanding user behavior (funnels, retention); decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Amplitude if your project is enterprise product analytics with advanced cohorts, medium lock-in is an acceptable trade-off, strong SDKs and docs (5/5) are a priority.
verdict_b: Choose Heap if your project is understanding user behavior (funnels, retention), a subscription starting at $1.7 fits your budget, medium lock-in is an acceptable trade-off.

faq.a: Amplitude uses pricing that starts at $61/mo (Plus) with free tier, and Heap uses subscription from $1.7. The pricing models are different, so a direct cheaper-than comparison depends on your volume and usage pattern. Amplitude offers a free tier; Heap does not.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Amplitude to Heap?
faq.a: Our data puts Amplitude at medium lock-in (proprietary data model, warehouse-native reduces lock-in), and Heap at medium lock-in (autocapture = proprietary format). Migration is feasible but not trivial — budget time for re-integration, data export, and parallel running before cutover.

Amplitude scores 5/5 on developer experience in our data, while Heap scores 4/5, so Amplitude has the edge on docs and SDK quality by that measure. That gap is reinforced by transparency scores of 4/5 versus 3/5. Still, run a small integration spike on both before deciding — team familiarity with a given SDK style often matters more than a one-point score gap.

faq.q: Is Heap a good alternative to Amplitude?
faq.a: Heap is a reasonable alternative to Amplitude when your workload leans more toward understanding user behavior (funnels, retention) than enterprise. The pricing model shifts too — Amplitude is pricing that starts at $61/mo (Plus) with free tier, Heap is subscription from $1.7 — so expect the cost profile to change as well. One caveat: Heap is explicitly not for simple projects, so check that constraint against your use-case before switching.
